title: Voicemail Settings
lede: Stop paying your AI agents to talk to answering machines. Voicemail Settings is your account-wide control room for voicemail detection: teach the classifier your customers' greetings, decide whether agents hang up or leave a message, and fine-tune detection timing — all in one place.

steps.title: Open the Voicemail Settings page
steps.content:
1. In the top navigation bar, click Contact Center.
  2. In the row of tabs that appears underneath, click Voicemail Settings (you may need to scroll the tab row to the right).

The page has up to three sections, top to bottom:

  • Voicemail Classifier Hints — optional phrases that help the AI recognize your voicemail greetings.
  • Override My Agents' Voicemail Settings — one switch to enforce the same voicemail behavior on every agent you own.
  • Override Sub-Account Agents — the same control applied to all of your sub-accounts' agents (this card only appears if you are a main account that has sub-accounts).
💡 The Save button at the top right applies to the whole page. Nothing you change takes effect until you click it.

images.alt: Voicemail Settings page under Contact Center with classifier hints and override panels
images.caption: Voicemail Settings lives under Contact Center. Save (top right) applies every change on the page.

images.alt: Voicemail Settings page under Contact Center with classifier hints and override panels
images.caption: Voicemail Settings lives under Contact Center. Save (top right) applies every change on the page.

steps.title: How voicemail detection works
steps.content:
When one of your agents places an outbound call and the line answers, an AI classifier listens to the first thing it hears — the greeting. Based on what it hears (a live "Hello?" versus "Please leave a message after the tone…"), it decides whether a human or a machine answered, and your agent reacts instantly: keep talking, hang up, or wait for the beep and leave a message.

  • It's automatic. Detection works out of the box for standard English voicemail and IVR greetings — no setup required.
  • Each agent has its own voicemail settings (in the agent's editor). This page lets you optionally override all of them at once, so you don't have to edit agents one by one.
  • Nothing here affects inbound calls — voicemail detection only matters when your agents are the ones dialing out (for example, dialer campaigns and callbacks).

steps.title: Add classifier hint phrases (optional)
steps.content:
The Voicemail Classifier Hints card lets you feed the classifier extra example phrases so it recognizes voicemail greetings specific to your market — Spanish greetings, regional carrier messages, or branded company greetings.

  1. Type a phrase you hear on your customers' voicemail greetings into the Add a classifier hint phrase... box — for example buzón de voz or your call has been forwarded.
  2. Click Add (or press Enter). The phrase appears as a tag above the box.
  3. To remove a phrase, click the × on its tag.
  4. Click Save when you're done.

The card title shows your usage against the limit (for example 24 / 50) — you can store up to 50 phrases. Once you hit the cap, the input is disabled until you remove a phrase.

💡 Hints are optional. If you leave the list empty, detection uses its built-in English-language examples — which is plenty for most accounts. Add hints when you call non-English-speaking regions or notice specific greetings slipping through as "human".

images.alt: Voicemail Classifier Hints card with phrase tags and an add box
images.caption: Each hint is a tag — add with the input box, remove with the ×. The title tracks your count against the 50-phrase limit.

images.alt: Voicemail Classifier Hints card with phrase tags and an add box
images.caption: Each hint is a tag — add with the input box, remove with the ×. The title tracks your count against the 50-phrase limit.

steps.title: Override voicemail settings for all your agents
steps.content:
Want every agent to behave the same way when it hits a voicemail — without opening each one? That's what the Override My Agents' Voicemail Settings card is for.

  1. Turn on the Enable Override switch.
  2. The override options expand below (covered in the next two steps).
  • Switch off (default): each agent uses the voicemail settings configured in its own editor.
  • Switch on: the settings in this card replace the voicemail configuration on all of your agents.
⚠️ While the override is on, changes to voicemail settings inside an individual agent's editor have no effect — this card wins. Turn the override off to hand control back to each agent.

images.alt: Override My Agents' Voicemail Settings card with the Enable Override switch off
images.caption: Enable Override replaces every agent's own voicemail configuration with the settings on this card.

images.alt: Override My Agents' Voicemail Settings card with the Enable Override switch off
images.caption: Enable Override replaces every agent's own voicemail configuration with the settings on this card.

steps.title: Choose what happens when voicemail is detected
steps.content:
With the override enabled, decide how agents should react to answering machines:

  1. Turn on Voicemail Detection. (Leave it off and overridden agents will simply keep talking as if a human answered — usually not what you want on outbound campaigns.)
  2. Under When voicemail is detected, pick one:
    • Hang up — the agent ends the call immediately. Best for high-volume dialing where a voicemail is just a "try again later".
    • Leave a message — the agent waits for the beep and speaks your message.
  3. If you chose Leave a message, type the message in the box that appears (for example: "Hi, this is Sarah from Acme calling about your service appointment — please call us back at your convenience.").
⚠️ Watch the character counter under the message box — voicemail messages are capped at 200 characters. If you go over, the agent only speaks the first 200.

images.alt: Override card expanded showing Voicemail Detection on, Leave a message selected, message text box and detection tuning fields
images.caption: Voicemail Detection on with Leave a message selected — the message box shows a live character count against the 200-character cap.

images.alt: Override card expanded showing Voicemail Detection on, Leave a message selected, message text box and detection tuning fields
images.caption: Voicemail Detection on with Leave a message selected — the message box shows a live character count against the 200-character cap.

steps.title: Fine-tune detection timing (advanced)
steps.content:
Below the behavior options, the Detection Tuning (Advanced) section exposes the timing thresholds the detector uses. All values are in seconds, every field shows its default underneath, and the defaults are right for almost everyone — only adjust these if you see misclassifications.

  • No-Speech Timeout (default 10s) — how long the detector waits for any audio after the call answers before giving up on a silent line. This clock starts after pickup, so it never counts ringing. Raise it if slow answerers stay silent after connecting.
  • Detection Timeout (default 20s) — the hard ceiling for the whole detection; when it elapses, the detector settles with whatever evidence it has. Keep it at or above the No-Speech Timeout.
  • Human Speech Threshold (default 2.5s) — greetings shorter than this (a quick "Hello?") are fast-tracked as human; longer greetings go to the AI classifier for a closer look.
  • Human Silence Threshold (default 0.5s) — how much silence after a short greeting confirms "human". Lower it to commit faster on quick pickups.
  • Machine Silence Threshold (default 1.5s) — how much silence after machine-like speech is needed before calling it a machine. Higher values give the classifier more of the greeting to work with.
  • Interrupt Agent on Machine (default on) — the instant a machine is detected, the agent stops talking, so it never talks over the greeting or the beep. Leave this on unless you have a specific reason not to.
💡 Rule of thumb: agents mistaking voicemails for humans → add classifier hints first (step 3). Silent lines getting dropped too early → raise the No-Speech Timeout.

images.alt: Detection Tuning section with No-Speech Timeout, Detection Timeout, speech and silence thresholds and the Interrupt Agent on Machine switch
images.caption: Every tuning field shows its default value underneath — reset a field by typing the default back in.

images.alt: Detection Tuning section with No-Speech Timeout, Detection Timeout, speech and silence thresholds and the Interrupt Agent on Machine switch
images.caption: Every tuning field shows its default value underneath — reset a field by typing the default back in.

steps.title: Override sub-account agents (main accounts only)
steps.content:
If you're a main account with sub-accounts, a second override card — Override Sub-Account Agents — appears at the bottom of the page.

  1. Turn on Enable Override in this card.
  2. Configure the same options as in step 5 and 6: Voicemail Detection, Hang up / Leave a message, and Detection Tuning.

This override is the strongest setting on the platform: it takes priority over both each sub-account's own override and their individual agent settings. Use it to guarantee consistent voicemail behavior across your whole organization from one screen.

images.alt: Override Sub-Account Agents card with its Enable Override switch
images.caption: The sub-account override outranks both the sub-account's own override and individual agent settings.

images.alt: Override Sub-Account Agents card with its Enable Override switch
images.caption: The sub-account override outranks both the sub-account's own override and individual agent settings.

steps.title: Save and verify on real calls
steps.content:
1. Click Save at the top right. A confirmation appears when your settings are stored.
  2. Place a test outbound call to a number you know goes to voicemail (your own mobile with the ringer off works great).
  3. Open Contact Center → Call Logs and check the call: it should show the agent detected the machine and either hung up or left your message.

That's it — from now on, every overridden agent handles answering machines exactly the way you decided, on every single call, automatically.

metaTitle: Voicemail Settings - Detection, Hints & Overrides | iMash Documentation
metaDescription: Configure account-wide voicemail detection for your AI agents: add classifier hint phrases, choose hang-up or leave-a-message behavior, tune detection timing, and override all agents or sub-accounts at once.
